title: Most of my losses came from trades that looked “valid”… until I double-checked them
author: Disastrous_Hotel_574
selftext:
I used to take trades that looked valid.

Clean enough.  
Good “feeling”.

Still lost.

So I reviewed a bunch of them.

The issue wasn’t direction.

It was structure:

* entries too early
* no clear invalidation
* setups that were almost right

That “almost” is expensive.

So I started doing one thing:

Before entering, I force a second check.

Not “does it look good?”  
But:

* is the level clear
* is the entry justified
* is the invalidation logical

If not → skip.

Way fewer bad trades.

Problem is doing this consistently.

So I built a small tool that analyzes a chart screenshot and gives a structured breakdown.

Acts like a second pair of eyes.

Curious how you guys validate your setups.

If you want to try it:  
[https://herculmarket.com](https://herculmarket.com/)
